source_jobs.title: Senior Director of Business Development - US Government
source_jobs.description:
QUINDAR

Quindar builds modern, cloud-native satellite mission operations software with a simple mission: to operate satellites like servers. Our platform replaces fragmented, legacy ground systems with an integrated, automation-driven approach that brings modern software velocity to space operations.

We're a small, fast-moving team with deep aerospace and software roots, energized by big challenges and motivated by the impact we have on real missions in this next chapter of the Space Age. If you want to work with sharp, ambitious people who push boundaries, move quickly, and are excited to take space operations to the next level, Quindar is the place to do it.

Senior Director of Business Development – U.S. Government
We are hiring a Senior Director of Business Development to help scale Quindar’s U.S. Government business. This is a senior leadership role responsible for defining and executing multi-year growth strategy, capturing high-value programs, and building a durable, repeatable revenue engine.

You will operate as a key business owner for the government segment alongside the Director of BD and working cross-functionally across product, engineering, and leadership to align market demand with company strategy.

What You’ll Do

Own the Government Business Line

 - Help define and execute Quindar’s multi-year strategy across DoD, USSF, SDA, and the IC

 - Own pipeline development, capture strategy, and program execution across priority opportunities

 - Make portfolio decisions on where to invest, compete, and partner

Drive Strategic Growth & Capture

 - Lead end-to-end capture for high-value programs, from early shaping through award

 - Influence requirements and acquisition strategy pre-RFP

 - Architect winning approaches across prime/sub positioning and teaming strategies

 - Deliver against revenue targets and strategic program milestones

Build and Scale the Growth Engine

 - Establish repeatable capture processes, pipeline discipline, and forecasting rigor

 - Build and lead a high-performing BD and capture team over time

 - Mentor and develop junior team members as the organization scales

Shape Market Position & Partnerships

 - Build and maintain senior relationships across government stakeholders (program offices, acquisition leaders, mission owners)

 - Develop and manage strategic partnerships with primes and ecosystem players

 - Position Quindar as a critical infrastructure layer for space operations

Influence Company Strategy

 - Translate government demand signals into product and roadmap priorities

 - Partner with product and engineering to align capabilities with mission needs

 - Help define pricing, packaging, and go-to-market strategy for government customers

What Success Looks Like

 - Secures key lighthouse programs and expands presence within priority accounts

 - Builds a high-quality, predictable pipeline aligned with company goals

 - Develops repeatable capture and partnership playbooks

 - Lays the foundation for a scalable government business unit

What We’re Looking For

 - 10+ years of experience in government BD, capture, or growth roles

 - Proven track record owning and winning complex programs ($50M+), including OTAs, BAAs, IDIQs, or equivalent

 - Deep understanding of DoD, USSF, SDA, and/or IC acquisition environments

 - Experience shaping opportunities pre-RFP and influencing program direction

 - Strong network with senior government stakeholders (program execs, acquisition leaders, mission owners)

 - Experience working with both primes and emerging technology vendors

 - Demonstrated ability to build and scale teams, processes, and pipeline infrastructure


CLEARANCE

 - Active TS/SCI clearance required.
   

Why This Role Matters

Quindar is moving from early adoption to scaled deployment across national security missions. This role will define how we win—and repeat—at that next stage.

You won’t just close deals. You’ll build the system that makes them predictable.

ITAR

source_jobs.title: Ground System Integration Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING 👨‍💻👩‍💻 🛰 🛰

You will be responsible for developing and integrating software Command & Control solutions for satellite vehicles as well as working with customer stakeholders to decompose mission requirements and engineer solutions with existing products. The successful candidate will have very strong software integration and debugging skills and deep experience in low level software integration with hardware (satellites or other!). You will bring or build expertise with C2 concepts and common industry standards and protocols such as CCSDS, CSP, CFDP, XTCE, and MIB. Your primary responsibility will be integrating new satellites and flight software into Quindar’s mission management platform and you will collaborate with a multidisciplinary team to do this while supporting development and integration of solutions into Quindar’s broader product suite. Ultimately, you will contribute to Quindar’s mission of simplifying operations and allowing our customers to utilize their satellites like servers!

This is a hybrid role with 2-3 days in-office in Arvada, CO. We aim to maximize benefits of having in-person collaboration spaces while also working on a flexible schedule with a large amount of remote work!




TECH SKILLS:

 - Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, C++, or Java

 - Experience developing real-time embedded software and/or integrating hardware assets with mission management software

 - Preferred experience with satellite communication protocols such as CCSDS, CSP, CFDP, IP and data processing algorithms

 - Preferred understanding of standardized command and telemetry definitions such as XTCE and MIB or similar concepts from other industries

 - Understanding of encryption algorithms such as AES-256 GCM, AES-256 CFB, AES-256 ECB, NSA Type 1 PEGASUS, NSA Type 1 CARIBOU

 - Preferred experience with AWS services such as Lambda, ECS, EKS, CloudWatch, S3, EFS/EBS, EC2, and RDS

 - Familiarity with Git and GitHub or similar framework for version control

 - Preference for experience with open source software


SOFT SKILLS:

 - Deep technical analysis and troubleshooting skills

 - Highly adaptable; you will integrate a wide variety of missions into Quindar’s software and will need to find creative solutions to work through unknowns and unforeseen challenges quickly

 - A foresight for scalability and proliferation of c2

 - Independent self-starter; able to complete projects on time with minimal guidance

 - A high standard for software maintainability, quality, resilience, and performance

 - Strong cross-functional communication and ability to distill complex information for internal and customer-facing audiences


BACKGROUND QUALIFICATIONS:

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or related field

 - 3+ years [professional] experience with satellite command and control systems or similarly complex hardware-software integration efforts

 - Ability to obtain a U.S. Security Clearance


ITAR REQUIREMENTS

 - To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR here https://www.pmddtc.state.gov/ddtc_public/ddtc_public?id=ddtc_kb_article_page&sys_id=24d528fddbfc930044f9ff621f961987.


WHY YOU'LL LOVE WORKING AT QUINDAR

 - We take work life balance very seriously. We require employees to take 15 days off but provide unlimited PTO and follow most US federal government holidays.

 - Mental health is just as important as physical so we provide quarterly health & wellness benefits.

 - Comprehensive health insurance for you and your family with 100% coverage for employees.

 - We encourage employees to save for retirement and provide 4% 401(k) matching.

 - Each year we have a 4

source_jobs.title: Vice President of Programs & Operations
source_jobs.description:
WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

Quindar is seeking a Vice President of Programs & Operations to lead mission execution across the company as we scale programs across commercial and national security customers.

This role is responsible for ensuring that Quindar consistently delivers mission-ready deployments to customers operating satellites in complex operational environments. The VP of Programs & Operations will own the systems, leadership, and execution infrastructure required to deliver customer programs reliably and at scale.

The successful candidate will operate as an internal execution leader, partnering closely with the CEO and leadership team to translate company strategy into operational delivery across engineering, product, and customer programs.

This role is particularly focused on supporting government and national security missions, ensuring Quindar can deliver reliable operational capability to customers operating in demanding environments.



RESPONSIBILITIES

Government and Customer Program Execution

 - Own execution of customer programs across commercial and government missions

 - Ensure delivery of program milestones, mission deployments, and operational readiness

 - Serve as the executive owner responsible for successful program delivery

 - Coordinate directly with government and commercial stakeholders to ensure mission success


MISSION DEPLOYMENT AND CUSTOMER ONBOARDING

 - Establish repeatable processes for integrating the Quindar platform into customer mission environments

 - Lead operational planning for new satellite missions and customer integrations

 - Reduce time required to onboard new missions onto the Quindar platform

 - Ensure integrations meet reliability, security, and operational requirements


PROGRAM MANAGEMENT AND CROSS-TEAM EXECUTION

 - Build and lead a program management organization responsible for coordinating delivery across engineering, product, and customer stakeholders

 - Implement structured program reviews, execution tracking, and delivery reporting

 - Drive cross-team accountability to ensure programs execute on schedule


OPERATIONAL INFRASTRUCTURE AND SCALING

 - Develop operational processes for deploying Quindar across multiple customer environments

 - Establish operational dashboards and reporting to provide visibility into program health and delivery timelines

 - Identify operational bottlenecks and implement systems that increase delivery speed and reliability


STRATEGIC OPERATIONAL PLANNING

 - Partner with company leadership to ensure operational readiness for new customer programs and mission deployments

 - Translate company growth plans into operational requirements for staffing, infrastructure, and execution

 - Support expansion into new government and commercial mission environments


TEAM LEADERSHIP

 - Recruit and lead program management and operational staff as the company grows

 - Establish a culture of ownership, urgency, and accountability across operational teams

 - Build the operational infrastructure required to support large-scale satellite mission deployments


QUALIFICATIONS

Required Experience

 - 10+ years of experience leading programs, operations, or delivery organizations

 - Currently possesses and is able to maintain an active U.S. TS/SCI with Poly

 - Experience working in a fast-paced organization or startup

 - Demonstrated experience delivering complex software or software/hardware systems

 - Experience supporting government, aerospace, or national security programs

 - Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams delivering mission-critical systems

 - Experience building operational processes in fast-growing organizations


PREFERRED BACKGROUND

 - Experience in aerospace, satellite operations, defense technology, or national security programs

 - Experience working with government customers including DoD or intelligence community organizations

 - Experience managing complex deployments across multiple stakeholders

 - Experie

source_jobs.title: Technical Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
About Quindar

Quindar builds modern software for satellite operations, helping teams operate spacecraft with greater speed, clarity, and reliability. We work at the intersection of aerospace, software, and operations, and we are growing thoughtfully as we scale.


About the Role

Quindar is looking for a Technical Recruiter to help us find, engage, and support exceptional talent across engineering, product, operations, aerospace, software, and other mission-aligned roles.

You’ll focus on technical sourcing, outbound recruiting, first-round candidate screens, pipeline development, interview coordination, and hiring manager partnership. You’ll also play a meaningful role in people operations, including onboarding support, recruiting administration, employee documentation, and company programs that help new hires and employees feel set up for success.

What You’ll Do

 - Build targeted pipelines for technical, operational, and mission-aligned roles.

 - Source and engage candidates through LinkedIn, communities, job boards, referrals, university networks, and other relevant channels.

 - Partner with hiring managers on role requirements, target profiles, sourcing priorities, and screening criteria.

 - Conduct first-round candidate screens to assess role alignment, background, motivation, and mission fit.

 - Support outbound recruiting campaigns, including research, outreach, follow-up, and pipeline tracking.

 - Coordinate interviews and keep candidates, hiring managers, and interview teams moving through the process.

 - Maintain accurate recruiting data, job postings, applicant tracking workflows, and candidate records.

 - Support offer-stage logistics, preboarding, onboarding, documentation, and first-day readiness for new hires.

 - Assist with people operations administration, company events, team programs, and offsites.

What We’re Looking For

 - Experience in technical recruiting, sourcing, talent acquisition, recruiting coordination, people operations, or a similar role.

 - Comfort finding, engaging, and screening candidates across technical, operational, aerospace, software, or mission-driven talent markets.

 - Ability to translate role requirements into clear sourcing strategies, candidate profiles, and screening questions.

 - Strong research instincts, organization, judgment, and follow-through.

 - Discretion with confidential candidate and employee information.

 - Comfort using applicant tracking systems, LinkedIn Recruiter, Slack, sourcing tools, HRIS tools, or similar platforms.

 - Interest in startups, aerospace, software, and mission-driven technology.

Nice to Have

 - Experience sourcing or screening for engineering, product, operations, aerospace, satellite operations, or software roles.

 - Familiarity with Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, Gem, HireEZ, or similar recruiting tools.

 - Experience supporting onboarding, preboarding, employee documentation, or people operations workflows.

 - Experience running outbound recruiting campaigns from research through pipeline tracking.

 - Experience partnering directly with hiring managers in a startup or high-growth environment.

 - Interest in building inclusive, scalable recruiting and people practices.

ITAR Requirements

To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR here.

Why you'll love working at Quindar

 - We work in a cutting edge industry and you will get the opportunity to be part of a small team with a large direct impact on the success of our customers’ space missions!

 - We take work life balance very seriously. We require employees to take 15 days off but provide unlimited PTO and follow most US federal government holidays.

 - Mental health is

source_jobs.title: Site Reliability Engineer (SRE)
source_jobs.description:
WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

Design, automate, deploy, and operate highly reliable cloud systems supporting mission-critical workloads for U.S. Government customers. This role is centered on DevSecOps and site reliability engineering, with a strong emphasis on deployment automation, operational stability, and system resilience across AWS GovCloud and AWS C2E environments.

You will be responsible for continuously improving the reliability and operability of Quindar’s platform in production, ensuring systems are observable, fault-tolerant, and require minimal manual intervention. Your work will directly impact mission success by improving system uptime, deployment velocity, and operational confidence in constrained and classified environments.

A key focus of this role is building and evolving automated deployment pipelines, hardened runtime environments, and repeatable infrastructure patterns that support secure and scalable operations in regulated environments.

You will also support and improve Quindar deployments to air-gapped networks, driving consistency, reliability, and performance across all environments. As the organization grows, you will help define and implement best practices for availability, latency, incident response, and service-level objectives (SLOs).

This role includes participation in incident response and a 24/7 on-call rotation, with a strong mandate to eliminate toil through automation and continuously improve system reliability.

You will collaborate closely with frontend, backend, and platform engineers to ensure systems meet performance, reliability, and mission assurance requirements.




TECHNICAL SKILLS

 - Strong experience with Kubernetes and containerized workloads in production environments

 - Hands-on experience operating clusters in AWS EKS, Rancher, or similar platforms

 - Experience supporting GovCloud, IL-enclave, or C2E environments

 - Deep experience with CI/CD systems and deployment automation (GitLab preferred)

 - Proficiency in Python and Infrastructure-as-Code tools (Terraform or similar)

 - Experience with observability platforms (Grafana LGTM stack, Datadog, or equivalent)

 - Strong understanding of distributed systems, APIs, databases, caching, and event-driven architectures

 - Solid networking fundamentals (VPCs, VPNs, load balancers, TLS, service connectivity)

 - Experience with Linux/Unix systems

 - Familiarity with cloud security best practices, enclave boundaries, and secure system design

 - Experience with identity and access management (AWS IAM, Auth0, Keycloak, ICAM patterns)

 - Strong Git fundamentals and experience supporting deployments across multiple classification levels


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related field

 - 3+ years of professional experience as an SRE, DevOps, reliability, infrastructure, or platform engineer

 - Active U.S. Security Clearance preffered

 - Experience working toward ATO/authorization in federal, DoD, or IC environments preferred

 - Experience supporting deployments in GovCloud, C2S/C2E, or IL-enclave environments highly desirable


ITAR REQUIREMENTS

 - To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR here https://www.pmddtc.state.gov/ddtc_public/ddtc_public?id=ddtc_kb_article_page&sys_id=24d528fddbfc930044f9ff621f961987.


WHY YOU'LL LOVE WORKING AT QUINDAR

 - We work in a cutting edge industry and you will get the opportunity to be part of a small team with a large direct impact on the success of our customers’ space missions!

 - We take work life balance very seriously. We require employees to take 15 days off but provide unlimited PTO and follow most US federal government holidays.

 - Mental healt

source_jobs.title: Forward Deployed Engineer
source_jobs.description:
WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

Our customers run satellite missions on our software. Failure isn't an option. As a Forward Deployed Product Engineer, you make sure our software holds up at the edge, where it matters most.

You'll also spot what's missing, what could be simpler, and what should exist but doesn't yet, then turn those observations into product direction.

This is a product role as much as an engineering one. You'll work in the field, in ops centers, and alongside customers. You're a generalist who moves across the full stack, from infrastructure to frontend, and you use AI as leverage to ship faster. At Quindar, we believe the future belongs to engineers who use AI to move faster, build better, and shape the next generation of our products




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Configure and deploy our software in customer environments, ensuring reliability and performance across complex workflows.

 - Troubleshoot issues across backend APIs, cloud infrastructure, databases, and the frontend.

 - Travel to customer sites and embed with operations teams, building trusted technical relationships.

 - Prototype products and iterate quickly with live customer data and direct feedback.

 - Act as the voice of the customer in product discussions, translating field insights into roadmap priorities, solutions, and integrations.

 - Partner with Product & Engineering to turn successful prototypes into production code.

 - Develop playbooks for repeatable integrations and maintain clear documentation and training resources.


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Education:
   
   - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Aerospace, Engineering, or a related field.

 - Experience:
   
   - 3+ years of experience in software engineering, with a focus on customer-facing roles.
   
   - Proven track record of building, deploying, or supporting production software in mission-critical environments.

 - Technical Skills:
   
   - Proficiency in Python; experience with TypeScript, Java, or similar is a plus.
   
   - Experience building software with AI tools such as Cursor, Amp, ChatGPT
   
   - Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud), databases, and containerization technologies (Docker, Kubernetes).

 - Soft Skills:
   
   - Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
   
   - Strong product sense: ability to question requirements, spot patterns, solve problems for the users.
   
   - Bias toward action: comfortable shipping prototypes quickly and iterating.
   
   - Adaptability and curiosity, thriving in ambiguous, fast-changing environments.

 - Certifications:
   
   - Relevant certifications (e.g., AWS Certified Solutions Architect, Certified Kubernetes Administrator) are a plus.

 - Active US Security Clearance (TS/SCI) required


ITAR REQUIREMENTS

 - To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR here https://www.pmddtc.state.gov/ddtc_public/ddtc_public?id=ddtc_kb_article_page&sys_id=24d528fddbfc930044f9ff621f961987.


WHY YOU'LL LOVE WORKING AT QUINDAR

 - We work in a cutting edge industry and you will get the opportunity to be part of a small team with a large direct impact on the success of our customers’ space missions!

 - We take work life balance very seriously. We require employees to take 15 days off but provide unlimited PTO and follow most US federal government holidays.

 - Mental health is just as important as physical so we provide quarterly health & wellness benefits.

 - Comprehensive health insurance for you and your family with 100% coverage for employees.

 - We encourage employees to save for retirement and provide 4% 401(k) matching.

 - Each year we have an annual company offsite. Previous locations include San

source_jobs.title: Vice President of Growth & Strategic Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
QUINDAR

Quindar builds modern, cloud-native satellite mission operations software with a simple mission: to operate satellites like servers. Our platform replaces fragmented, legacy ground systems with an integrated, automation-driven approach that brings modern software velocity to space operations.

We're a small, fast-moving team with deep aerospace and software roots, energized by big challenges and motivated by the impact we have on real missions in this next chapter of the Space Age. If you want to work with sharp, ambitious people who push boundaries, move quickly, and are excited to take space operations to the next level, Quindar is the place to do it.



VICE PRESIDENT OF GROWTH & STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIPS

Quindar is hiring a Vice President of Growth & Strategic Partnerships to own company-wide growth and revenue outcomes, with a primary focus on U.S. Department of Defense and Intelligence Community adoption. This position will enable operational value, drive integration gravity, and accelerate program pull-through with government (B2B) and commercial relationships (B2B2G).

This is a senior executive role accountable for opening doors, leading strategic capture and partnership efforts, and building repeatable GTM systems that scale Quindar into the modern ground software platform for space operations.



WHAT YOU’LL DO


REVENUE & GROWTH OWNERSHIP

 - Own Quindar’s growth strategy and revenue outcomes across government and strategic
   commercial markets.

 - Build predictable pipeline generation and execution systems for long-cycle government and
   enterprise deals.

 - Drive expansion, renewals, and program pull-through into enduring customer relationships.


COMMERCIAL → GOVERNMENT FLYWHEEL LEADERSHIP

 - Convert commercial traction into DoD/IC adoption and programs of record.

 - Leverage commercial credibility to accelerate trust and integration gravity within
   government missions.

 - Define segmentation and GTM pathways that align commercial wins with national security
   expansion.


DEFENSE & INTELLIGENCE RELATIONSHIP LEADERSHIP

 - Bring and deepen relationships across DoD, IC, Space Force, SDA, NRO ecosystem, primes,
   and integrators.

 - Serve as a front-line executive presence with senior mission owners and acquisition
   stakeholders.

 - Open new teaming partnerships and strategic channels that accelerate adoption.



DEAL EXECUTION & CAPTURE MOTION

 - Lead complex deal strategy, pricing, packaging, and contracting across FAR/OTA/subcontracting pathways.

 - Partner closely with Sales, Product, and Engineering to win technically complex mission-
   critical deals.

 - Improve win rates, deal velocity, and expansion within strategic accounts.

 


MARKETING AS FORCE MULTIPLICATION

 - Partner with Marketing to deploy demand generation, thought leadership, and account-based air cover in support of BD and capture.

 - Build credibility and awareness within the defense space ecosystem through targeted
   campaigns and events.
   
   


PARTNERSHIPS & ECOSYSTEM SCALE

 - Build and scale strategic partnerships with primes, integrators, ground system providers,
   and infrastructure partners.

 - Structure agreements that create durable distribution channels and mission pull-through.



TEAM BUILDING & SCALE

 - Build and lead a high-performing growth organization over time (BD, partnerships, revenue
   operations).

 - Create internal systems that allow Quindar to scale beyond founder-led growth.



WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 10+ years of GTM, BD, partnerships, or growth leadership in aerospace, defense, or national
   security space.

 - Active TS/SCI clearance

 - Existing credibility and relationships across DoD/IC space stakeholders and defense prime
   ecosystems.

 - Demonstrated success closing complex mission-critical software deals and strategic
   partnerships.

 - Experience navigating government acquisition pathways and long-cycle capture motions.

 - Deep understanding of space operations,

source_jobs.title: Mission Integration Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Quindar is seeking a Mission Integration Engineer to help define, architect, and deliver integrated satellite ground systems for commercial and government customers.

The Mission Integration team owns the technical path from opportunity shaping through verified mission deployment. This includes solutions and sales engineering, technical proposal development, solution architecture, product strategy input, and formal systems engineering functions during execution. Mission Integration Engineers become deep subject matter experts on both Quindar’s platform and the customer’s mission, translating mission needs into executable technical solutions and ensuring those solutions are delivered, verified, and accepted.

This is a highly cross-functional role. You will work closely with both customers and Quindar teams in sales, product, software engineering, forward-deployed engineering, and customer success. You will help shape contract scope, define mission architectures, identify product gaps, manage requirements and verification outcomes, and ensure Quindar’s software is aligned to the mission’s operational, technical, and programmatic needs.

We are looking for candidates who can operate at both the big-picture and detailed levels: someone who can understand a customer opportunity deeply, reason through architecture and trade space, communicate clearly with technical and non-technical stakeholders, and drive structure in complex, first-of-kind missions.

 


KEY RESPONSIBILITIES


MISSION ARCHITECTURE & OPPORTUNITY SHAPING

 - Serve as a technical subject matter expert for customer opportunities, deeply understanding the customer’s mission, CONOPS, operational needs, interfaces, constraints, and success criteria.

 - Lead solution architecture for commercial and government opportunities.

 - Partner with sales and business development teams to shape deal strategy, technical scope, proposal approach, and customer-facing solution narratives.

 - Support technical proposal development, including RFI/RFP responses, compliance matrices, architecture descriptions, technical volumes, BOEs, demos, and customer design reviews.

 - Translate customer mission needs into clear technical solutions that align Quindar’s platform capabilities, product roadmap, engineering capacity, and delivery approach.

 - Identify product gaps, integration risks, and architectural tradeoffs early in the opportunity lifecycle and drive alignment across internal teams.

 - Support customer meetings, technical interchange meetings, site visits, conferences, and program reviews as needed.


SYSTEMS ENGINEERING & MISSION INTEGRATION

 - Own the systems engineering process and outcomes for assigned missions, with the level of rigor tailored to the customer and program.

 - Lead or coordinate requirements development, requirements management, interface definition, architecture documentation, verification planning, and acceptance criteria.

 - Own the verification and validation process across mission threads, including commanding, telemetry, automation, mission data flows, operational workflows, and mission-specific analytics.

 - Coordinate V&V execution across product, software engineering, forward-deployed engineering, customer, and program stakeholders.

 - Develop and maintain mission-relevant systems engineering artifacts such as requirements documents, ICDs, CONOPS, verification matrices, test plans, acceptance plans, and architecture models.

 - Support more formal systems engineering approaches for government programs, including traceability, documentation discipline, MBSE, and structured technical reviews.

 - Ensure technical decisions are clearly documented, communicated, and aligned across customer, engineering, product, and delivery teams.


PRODUCT STRATEGY & PLATFORM FEEDBACK

 - Advise product strategy and roadmap direction based on recurring customer needs, mission patterns, and technical gaps observed across opportunities and integrations.

 - Disting

source_jobs.title: Principal Engineer, Mission Software
source_jobs.description:
WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

We’re looking for a Principal Engineer, Mission Systems to lead Quindar’s Command & Control (C2) and Ground Systems Integration teams. This role combines technical leadership, software and systems architecture, and people management to deliver mission-critical capabilities for commercial and government space operations.

You’ll lead a focused team of engineers building world-class command and control software while also serving as a systems and software architect on strategic customer programs that apply Quindar’s platform to complex government and defense use cases. This role requires deep technical expertise, strong program leadership, and the ability to bridge product development with mission-specific integration efforts.

This is a hybrid role with 3 days in office. We aim to maximize the benefits of in-person collaboration while maintaining flexibility through a remote-friendly work environment.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Lead and mentor a multidisciplinary engineering team focused on Command & Control (C2) software and Ground Systems Integration capabilities

 - Drive technical execution across mission systems programs, balancing delivery timelines, system reliability, and long-term architectural scalability

 - Serve as system and software architect for strategic government and defense programs leveraging Quindar’s platform

 - Define and evolve software architecture across distributed systems, mission operations tooling, integration layers, and operator-facing applications

 - Collaborate closely with product, mission operations, customer success, and executive leadership to translate mission requirements into scalable technical solutions

 - Guide the team through complex integration efforts involving third-party systems, mission partners, and government stakeholders

 - Maintain hands-on involvement in technical design and software development, contributing directly to architecture reviews, implementation strategy, and critical code paths

 - Support hiring and organizational growth for mission software engineering functions

 - Mentor engineers and technical leads, helping develop technical depth, ownership, and leadership across the organization

 - Coordinate with Engineering and Product leadership to proactively identify architectural gaps, technical risks, and cross-team dependencies

 - Contribute to engineering standards, operational excellence, incident response processes, and post-mortem reviews

 - Communicate technical concepts, program status, and architectural decisions clearly across technical and non-technical stakeholders


QUALIFICATIONS

 - Proven experience leading complex software engineering efforts in aerospace, defense, mission operations, autonomy, robotics, or other high-reliability domains

 - Strong software architecture experience across distributed systems, backend infrastructure, APIs, operator tooling, and mission-critical applications

 - Experience serving as a technical lead, systems architect, or chief engineer on large-scale customer or government programs

 - Demonstrated ability to lead and mentor engineering teams while remaining deeply technical and hands-on

 - Experience integrating software systems across multiple teams, vendors, or external mission partners

 - Strong understanding of software development life cycles, systems engineering principles, and Agile development methodologies

 - Ability to code and review production-quality code in languages relevant to Quindar’s technology stack

 - Excellent communication and leadership skills with the ability to align technical execution with strategic mission objectives

 - Strong organizational and program leadership skills, with the ability to manage multiple priorities and drive execution across parallel efforts

 - Experience working with government customers, defense programs, or regulated environments is a strong plus

 - Ability to obtain U.S. Security Clearance (Active clearance strongly preferr

source_jobs.title: IT Support Administrator
source_jobs.description:
Quindar is seeking an IT Support Administrator to support day-to-day IT operations across corporate and secure operational environments. This role provides technical support for end users, supports endpoint and identity/account administration, and helps maintain internal IT systems and services.

The ideal candidate has experience supporting enterprise IT environments, strong troubleshooting skills, and a customer-focused mindset. This position is well-suited for someone with 2–3 years of experience who is looking to grow in an aerospace, defense, or secure mission-operations environment.

Work Environment

This position is primarily onsite to support operational requirements. The candidate will be expected to work onsite four (4) days per week during normal business hours (9:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m.), with one (1) remote workday based on operational needs.


Essential Responsibilities

 - Provide Tier 1/Tier 2 technical support for employees primarily in macOS environments, with the flexibility to expand support to Linux and Windows systems as needed

 - Troubleshoot and resolve hardware, software, networking, and user access issues; escalate appropriately and follow issues through to closure

 - Support onboarding and offboarding activities, including account provisioning, device setup, and access management

 - Maintain endpoint management solutions (enrollment, policies, patching, and compliance posture)

 - Assist with administration of identity and access management systems, including MFA, SSO, and role-based access controls (RBAC)

 - Support office IT infrastructure, including VPN connectivity, Wi-Fi, printers, and conferencing systems

 - Document work performed, update internal knowledge base articles, and maintain accurate asset inventory records


Basic Qualifications

 - 2–3 years of experience supporting enterprise or corporate IT environments

 - Experience providing desktop/end-user support in macOS and Windows environments

 - Basic familiarity with Linux operating systems

 - Understanding of networking fundamentals, including TCP/IP, VPNs, DNS, DHCP, and wireless networking

 - Familiarity with identity and access management concepts (e.g., MFA, SSO, least privilege)

 - Strong troubleshooting, organizational, and communication skills

 - Ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced environment

 - Ability to work onsite in support of operational requirements


Additional Details

 - On-call/after-hours support: As needed for incidents, planned maintenance, or special events

 - Travel: Minimal; may include local travel between sites as required

 - Physical requirements: Ability to lift and move IT equipment (up to 30 lbs.)

 - Employment eligibility: Must be authorized to work in the United States; position may require the ability to obtain and maintain a U.S. security clearance

Preferred Qualifications

 - Experience with endpoint management platforms such as Jamf, Intune, or Kandji

 - Familiarity with enterprise collaboration and productivity platforms including Google Workspace, Okta, Slack, Atlassian, or Microsoft 365

 - Experience supporting regulated or compliance-driven environments

 - Prior experience supporting defense, aerospace, or government customers

 - Active U.S. Security Clearance or ability to obtain and maintain one


Desired Characteristics

 - Strong customer-service mindset with a focus on responsiveness and reliability

 - Willingness to learn secure infrastructure and mission operations support processes to grow in the organization

 - Strong attention to detail and documentation practices

 - Collaborative approach to supporting engineering and operational teams
   
   


ITAR REQUIREMENTS

 - To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizati

source_jobs.title: Backend Engineer
source_jobs.description:
QUINDAR

Quindar builds modern, cloud-native satellite mission operations software with a simple mission: to operate satellites like servers. Our platform replaces fragmented, legacy ground systems with an integrated, automation-driven approach that brings modern software velocity to space operations.

We're a small, fast-moving team with deep aerospace and software roots, energized by big challenges and motivated by the impact we have on real missions in this next chapter of the Space Age. If you want to work with sharp, ambitious people who push boundaries, move quickly, and are excited to take space operations to the next level, Quindar is the place to do it.



WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING 👨‍💻👩‍💻 🛰 🛰

You will be responsible for developing ground satellite operations software using Python and AWS services. Don't worry, you do not need to be an expert in satellites or understand space! Satellites are just flying servers you can connect to every 90 minutes but we connect to hundreds of them.

You will develop APIs, integrate with AWS services such as EC2, S3, RDS, Fargate, and EKS with a focus on scalability, security, cost, and performance of the software. Working closely with cross-functional teams to understand requirements, you will implement new features and troubleshoot issues. Your strong knowledge of AWS services and best practices will enable you to optimize resource utilization and automate processes, ultimately contributing to the efficient and reliable operations of thousands of satellites.

You will primarily be responsible for developing backend software capabilities, but the ideal candidate can contribute across backend software development, generative AI integrations, DevSecOps and CI/CD tooling, and software architecture design and maintenance.

Day to day, you will be a part of the engineering team of frontend and backend engineers working in an Agile Scrum environment.

Tech Skills

 - Proficiency in Python, GraphQL, Docker, and Terraform (or relevant Infrastructure-as-Code tools)

 - Experience designing, analyzing, and troubleshooting APIs of AI platforms such as OpenAI, Anthropic, and AskSage

 - Strong knowledge of containerized applications

 - Familiarity with Kubernetes cluster deployments

 - Experience with AWS services such as Lambda, ECS, EKS, CloudWatch, S3, EFS/EBS, EC2, and RDS

 - Fluency in collaborative development using Git with platforms like GitHub or GitLab

Soft Skills

 - Deep technical analysis and troubleshooting skills

 - Independent self-starter; able to complete projects on time with minimal guidance

 - A high standard for software maintainability, quality, resilience, and performance

 - Strong cross-functional communication between frontend and backend engineers and product managers

Background Qualifications

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science (or related field)

 - 3+ years professional experience in backend development, full stack development or other related role. Professional experience can be replaced with a self-starter who has drive, the technical skills, and is obsessed with their product.

 - U.S. Person status required – we are not able to sponsor visas at this time.

 - Ability to obtain U.S. Security Clearance

ITAR REQUIREMENTS

 - To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR here https://www.pmddtc.state.gov/ddtc_public/ddtc_public?id=ddtc_kb_article_page&sys_id=24d528fddbfc930044f9ff621f961987.


WHY YOU'LL LOVE WORKING AT QUINDAR

 - We work in a cutting edge industry and you will get the opportunity to be part of a small team with a large direct impact on the success of our customers’ space missions!

 - We take work life balance very seriously. We require em

source_jobs.title: Staff Engineer, Ground Systems
source_jobs.description:
WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

We are seeking a Staff Engineer, Ground Systems to serve as a senior technical leader supporting the successful engineering execution of complex satellite ground systems programs. This role combines program-level technical leadership, system-level engineering coordination, and hands-on technical contribution across Quindar’s mission operations software ecosystem.

The Staff Engineer, Ground Systems acts as a technical integrator and program engineering leader across mission software, platform infrastructure, operations, reliability, security, and mission integration activities. This role is responsible for helping ensure technical alignment, architectural cohesion, and successful mission execution across complex programs while partnering closely with software / system architects and domain engineering leads who retain ownership of implementation and execution within their respective areas.

Successful candidates often come from backgrounds in satellite ground systems, mission operations, or other complex distributed software systems with a focus on both defense and commercial space operations.

The ideal candidate combines strong software and systems understanding with operational awareness and the ability to lead technical execution across multidisciplinary engineering teams.




KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Serve as a program technical leader / Chief Engineer for complex commercial and government space programs

 - Support successful engineering execution across mission software, platform infrastructure, reliability, security, and integration activities

 - Coordinate technical alignment across engineering teams to ensure cohesive system behavior and successful mission execution

 - Partner with engineering leads to identify and resolve cross-system technical risks, interface challenges, and integration concerns

 - Support architectural cohesion and long-term technical evolution across Quindar’s ground systems ecosystem

 - Contribute to technical strategy, system design, and architecture development for mission operations platforms

 - Participate in proposal development, technical solution definition, and customer technical engagements

 - Conduct technical reviews, design reviews, and mission readiness assessments

 - Support anomaly investigation, troubleshooting, and operational issue resolution for mission-critical systems

 - Provide mentorship and technical guidance to engineers across the organization

 - Contribute directly to engineering efforts through design, implementation, prototyping, and technical problem solving
   
   


QUALIFICATIONS

 - 8+ years of experience developing, integrating, or operating aerospace, satellite, or mission-critical software systems

 - Strong technical breadth across software systems, operational systems, and aerospace mission domains

 - Experience with satellite ground systems, mission operations, command and control, or flight dynamics and mission planning tools

 - Experience designing, integrating, or operating distributed software systems in production environments

 - Strong understanding of software architecture, systems integration, and operational reliability

 - Ability to communicate effectively across software, operations, platform / infrastructure, and customer stakeholders

 - Experience leading technical efforts across multidisciplinary engineering teams

 - Comfortable operating in ambiguous and rapidly evolving technical environments

 - Active U.S. Security Clearance (TS/SCI Preferred, S Required)


ITAR REQUIREMENTS

 - To conform to U.S. Government export regulations, applicant must be a (i) U.S. citizen or national, (ii) U.S. lawful, permanent resident (aka green card holder), (iii) Refugee under 8 U.S.C. § 1157, or (iv) Asylee under 8 U.S.C. § 1158, or be eligible to obtain the required authorizations from the U.S. Department of State. Learn more about the ITAR here https://www.pmddtc.state.gov/ddtc_public/ddtc_public?id=ddtc_kb_article_pa

source_jobs.title: CMMC - Information Security System Officer (ISSO)
source_jobs.description:
QUINDAR

Quindar builds modern, cloud-native satellite mission operations software with a simple mission: to operate satellites like servers. Our platform replaces fragmented, legacy ground systems with an integrated, automation-driven approach that brings modern software velocity to space operations.

We're a small, fast-moving team with deep aerospace and software roots, energized by big challenges and motivated by the impact we have on real missions in this next chapter of the Space Age. If you want to work with sharp, ambitious people who push boundaries, move quickly, and are excited to take space operations to the next level, Quindar is the place to do it.



WHAT YOU WILL BE DOING 🧑‍💻

The Information Systems Security Officer (ISSO) is responsible for enforcing and maintaining information security policies, standards, and procedures to ensure the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information systems.



The ISSO will uphold requirements in NIST SP 800-171 to protect Quindar Business Systems that process, transmit, or store Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI) and maintain Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) 2.0 accreditation. The ISSO will coordinate directly with the Quindar ISSM, FSO, System Administrators, Leadership, and the General User community to foster strong security culture and ensure compliance with governing Federal Regulations.



Key Responsibilities:

 1. Operations Support
    
    - Review user requests in conjunction with ISSM and System Administrators to determine security impacts of software additions or configuration changes to systems.
    
    - Onboard users to systems, including development and delivery of training and briefings of Roles and Responsibilities operating on systems.

 2. Risk Management and Assessment:
    
    - Conduct regular risk assessments and vulnerability assessments to identify potential security threats.
    
    - Implement risk mitigation strategies and manage the risk management framework.

 3. Continuous Monitoring and Audit Management:
    
    - Prepare for and execute both self-assessments and external assessments with Government Security Control Assessors in support of achieving and maintaining CMMC accreditation.
    
    - Execute Continuous Monitoring activities of employed security controls to ensure comprehensive and effective implementation over time, including but not limited to analyzing user/system audit logs, malware protections, vulnerability reporting, and access reviews.

 4. Incident Response and Management:
    
    - Develop and maintain an Incident Response Plan, partnering with Government Customers/Prime/Subcontractors for reporting procedures.
    
    - Lead incident response activities, including investigation, containment, and remediation of security incidents.
    
    - Investigate and adjudicate SIEM events.

 5. System Security Plans (SSPs):
    
    - Maintain System Security Plans and collecting all required artifacts (Compliance and Vulnerability reports, documented Policies/Procedures, etc.)
    
    - Ensure that SSPs are regularly reviewed, updated, and compliant with regulatory requirements.

 6. Collaboration and Communication:
    
    - Work closely with System Administrators, compliance, and other departments to ensure cohesive and comprehensive security strategies.
    
    - Serve as a point of contact for security-related issues and provide guidance and support to other teams.

 7. Continuous Improvement:
    
    - Stay up-to-date with the latest security trends, technologies, and regulatory requirements.
    
    - Continuously improve security measures and processes to protect information systems effectively.

Qualifications:

 - Citizenship: US Citizenship

 - Clearance: Eligibility for a US Government Security Clearance

 - Education: Bachelor’s degree in Information Security, Computer Science, or a related field.

Experience:

 - 2+ years of experience in information security, working

source_jobs.title: Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Quindar

Quindar builds modern software for satellite operations, helping teams operate spacecraft with greater speed, clarity, and reliability. We work at the intersection of aerospace, software, and operations, supporting customers who need dependable tools for complex, real-world missions.

About the Role

Quindar is hiring a Full Stack Engineer to develop ground satellite operations software using Python, AWS, and modern web technologies. In this role, you will support mission-unique software development for U.S. government programs, working across backend and frontend systems while also contributing to Quindar’s core product.

You do not need to be a satellite expert to succeed here. We are looking for a strong software engineer who is excited to build reliable, scalable systems in a mission-driven environment.

This is a hybrid role based in Arvada, CO.


What You’ll Do

 - Build and maintain backend services, APIs, and cloud-native applications for satellite operations workflows.

 - Develop mission-specific software for U.S. government customers while contributing to Quindar’s core product platform.

 - Design, integrate, and troubleshoot services using AWS technologies such as EC2, S3, RDS, Fargate, EKS, CloudWatch, S3, EFS/EBS, EC2, and RDS.

 - Work across backend and frontend systems, including integration with React, TypeScript, GraphQL, and modern application frameworks.

 - Improve the scalability, security, cost efficiency, reliability, and performance of software used in satellite operations.

 - Build and support containerized and serverless architectures using tools such as Docker, Kubernetes, Terraform, and CI/CD pipelines.

 - Collaborate with satellite operators, customers, product managers, frontend engineers, backend engineers, and other cross-functional partners to understand requirements and deliver new capabilities.

 - Troubleshoot production issues, analyze system behavior, and improve maintainability, resilience, and observability over time.

 - Automate development, deployment, and operational workflows to help teams move quickly and reliably.

What We’re Looking For

 - Active Secret clearance.

 - 4+ years of professional experience in backend development, full-stack engineering, DevOps, or a related software engineering role.

 - Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, a related technical field, or equivalent practical experience.

 - Strong proficiency with Python and experience building production backend services.

 - Experience designing, analyzing, and troubleshooting APIs, distributed systems, relational and NoSQL databases, caching systems, event-driven systems, and multi-tier architectures.

 - Experience with AWS services and cloud-native development patterns.

 - Hands-on experience with Docker, containerized applications, and Kubernetes systems.

 - Experience with infrastructure as code tools such as Terraform or similar.

 - Familiarity with JavaScript or TypeScript, with experience either developing full-stack or frontend software or closely integrating with frontend engineers.

 - Experience with version control systems such as Git, GitHub, GitLab, or similar tools.

 - Strong technical analysis, debugging, and troubleshooting skills.

 - Ability to work independently, manage projects with minimal guidance, and deliver high-quality work on time.

 - High standards for software maintainability, quality, resilience, security, and performance.

 - Strong communication skills across engineering, product, customer, and operations stakeholders.

Nice to Have

 - Experience building React components in TypeScript.

 - Experience with frameworks such as Next.js, Refine, or Vite.

 - Experience with Kubernetes and AWS services such as EKS, ECS, CloudWatch, S3, EFS/EBS, EC2, and RDS.

 - Experience building CI/CD pipelines, especially with GitHub Actions.

 - Experience working on satellite operations, aerospace, defense, or government software systems.

 - Demonstrated self-starting

notes: Quindar is a satellite operations software company with a strong engineering focus, hiring for various technical roles, including backend, full stack, and DevSecOps engineers.
